data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: ZM, NIO, BJ, and more
data.description:
Stock futures ticked higher on Friday as investors awaited Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s speech at Jackson Hole, looking for clues on the outlook for monetary policy.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Zoom Communications (NASDAQ:ZM) +9% - Shares rose after delivering strong Q2 results and raising its full-year outlook. The company guided Q3 revenue to $1.21B–$1.22B (midpoint $1.215B vs. $1.21B est.) and EPS to $1.42–$1.44 (vs. $1.39 est.), while FY revenue was lifted to $4.83B–$4.84B (vs. prior $4.8B–$4.81B, $4.81B est.) and EPS to $5.81–$5.84 (vs. prior $5.56–$5.59, $5.60 est.). CEO Eric Yuan credited AI-driven innovation and efficiency gains, highlighting Zoom’s highest revenue growth in 11 quarters, a 9-pt GAAP operating margin expansion, and stronger free cash flow guidance of $1.74B–$1.78B.
  • NIO (NYSE:NIO) +8% - Shares surged after the company unveiled one of its most affordable vehicles yet, highlighting the intensifying price war in the domestic market. The new ES8 is offered in six- and seven-seat Executive Premium and Executive Signature Editions with starting prices of RMB 416,800 (RMB 308,800 with BaaS) and RMB 456,800 (RMB 348,800 with BaaS). The base ES8 is priced below the Tesla (TSLA) Model Y L six-seat SUV, and in Europe the model will be marketed as the EL8 due to a trademark dispute with Audi.
  • Biohaven (NYSE:BHVN) +6% - Shares jumped after the FDA canceled a planned advisory committee meeting tied to the review of its lead drug troriluzole for spinocerebellar ataxia, a rare neurodegenerative disorder. The biotech had received priority review for the NDA earlier this year, with the review timeline extended in May as regulators evaluated additional submissions. In an SEC filing, Biohaven disclosed that on Aug. 21 the FDA informed the company that an AdCom is no longer required, a move investors interpreted as a positive signal for approval prospects.
  •  

Biggest stock losers

  • Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) -8% - Shares slipped after issuing slower growth guidance for Q1 and FY2026, overshadowing better-than-expected Q4 results. The QuickBooks and Credit Karma parent sees FY2026 revenue of $21.0B-$21.2B, up 12%-13% from last year, roughly in line with consensus, and projects non-GAAP EPS of $22.98-$23.18 vs. $22.99 expected. By segment, Global Business Solutions revenue is forecast to rise 14%-15% from 16% in FY2025, Consumer Group up 8%-9% vs. 10%, and Credit Karma up 10%-13% vs. 32%. For fiscal Q1, Intuit guided EPS of $3.05-$3.12, bracketing the $3.07 consensus, on revenue growth of 14%-15% compared with 20% in Q4.
  • BJ’s Wholesale Club (BJ) -8% - Shares fell after posting mixed Q2 results, with total comparable club sales down 0.3% due to lower fuel prices, though ex-gas comps rose 2.3%. Membership fee income climbed 9% on higher acquisition, retention, and premium penetration. Operating income rose 6.3%, and adjusted EBITDA gained 8%. Looking ahead, BJ’s guided FY comparable sales ex-gas to increase 2%–3.5% and raised adjusted EPS outlook to $4.20–$4.35 vs. the prior view of $4.10–$4.30 and the consensus of $4.30.
  • Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) -6% - Shares dropped after reporting fiscal Q2 results and guidance that exceeded expectations but failed to spark enthusiasm. For Q3, the company guided subscription revenue of $2.24B, in line with estimates, while FY2026 subscription revenue is projected at $8.82B, slightly above Wall Street’s $8.8B forecast. Workday also raised its adjusted operating margin outlook to 29% from 28%, topping both its prior view and the 28.5% consensus. Alongside results, the company announced an agreement to acquire Paradox, an AI-powered candidate experience platform.

data.title: Stellantis bringing back the Jeep Cherokee
data.description:
Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) is reviving the Jeep Cherokee with added features and a turbo-four hybrid powertrain, aiming to reignite sales and strengthen its position in the U.S. SUV market.

“This is the biggest segment in the United States, and bringing this back into our portfolio is a perfect fit between the Compass and the Grand Cherokee,” Jeep CEO Bob Broderdorf said in an interview Friday morning on CNBC, adding that the decision to discontinue the Cherokee in 2023 was “obviously one of the largest misses from our sales decline.”

Former CEO Carlos Tavares shelved the Jeep Cherokee in 2023 as part of a cost-cutting initiative and to free up resources for EV production. Prior to the company’s decision to idle Cherokee production, sales of the vehicle were down ~61% for the first half of 2022.

But Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) is hoping the new Jeep will rekindle demand for the once iconic SUV with a new 1.6-liter turbo-four hybrid powertrain, 500 mile range on a single tank of gas, and a “technology-filled” interior that includes Stellantis’ new Connect One service package. The new model also has more interior and cargo space.

With a starting sticker price of $35,000, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) has priced the Jeep Cherokee to compete with popular SUVs like the Honda (HMC) CR-V EX, Buick (GM) SUV lineup, and Toyota (TM) RAV4. However, the vehicle will be made at Stellantis' (STLA) Toluca, Mexico facility as a result of the closure of its prior home in Belvedere, Illinois, making the car liable for import taxes into the U.S.

data.title: Estee Lauder declares $0.35 dividend
data.description:
• Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL) declared $0.35/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.58%
  • Payable Sept. 16; for shareholders of record Sept. 2; ex-div Sept. 2.
  • See EL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Exact Sciences withdraws injunction request for Cologuard rival
data.description:
• Exact Sciences (NASDAQ:EXAS) has withdrawn its request for a preliminary injunction tied to Genescopy's patent for its stool-based colorectal cancer screening test, ColoSense, a rival to Cologuard.
  • The motion, filed in July 2024, had aimed to block the launch and marketing of ColoSense.
  • According to Geneoscopy, the decision to pull back the motion aligns with its view that the injunction request was unfounded.
  • With the matter no longer pending, Genescopy said it remains free to broaden access to ColoSense for patients and healthcare providers nationwide.

data.title: Tempus AI announces $81.25M acquisition of Paige
data.description:
Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) announced on Friday an acquisition of the AI company specializing in digital pathology, Paige, for $81.25M.

The deal consideration will be paid predominantly in Tempus common stock, as well as an assumption of the target company's remaining commitment under its existing Microsoft Azure cloud services agreement.

Shares were +0.12% pre-market to $76.80.

data.title: Fed should ease if labor market risks outweigh inflation, Collins says
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins said Friday that if labor market risks start to outweigh inflation concerns, it would be appropriate for the U.S. central bank to ease back from its current "modestly restrictive" stance.

"The risks on the two sides [of the Fed's dual mandate] have come into rough in balance, Collins said in an interview with Bloomberg TV in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, citing elevated inflation and slowing employment growth.

With upside risks to inflation tied to tariffs and downside risks to jobs, Collins is backing the Fed's current rate stance remains appropriate at the elevated 4.25%-4.50% target range. 

At the same time, though, "we cannot wait until all of the uncertainty is behind us," the Boston Fed chief said. "You've got to make decisions in real time." She underscored how the Fed needs to balance the inflation and employment mandates to offset any stagflation risks.

data.title: Positron to redomicile to Delaware from Texas
data.description:
Medical technology company Positron (OTCPK:POSC) announced on Friday a strategic redomiciling to Delaware from Texas.

The redomiciling is aimed at enhancing the company’s corporate governance and legal framework as it prepares to file a Form 10 registration statement with the U.S. SEC to become a fully reporting public company.

Additionally, Positron established a new corporate headquarters in the Buffalo-Niagara Falls region.

data.title: Akzo Nobel rises after activist investor Cevian takes 3% stake
data.description:
Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) (OTCQX:AKZOY) +6% in European trading Friday after activist investor Cevian built a ~3% stake in the paints maker, according to a Dutch regulatory filing.

Based on Thursday's closing share price, Cevian’s stake in Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) (OTCQX:AKZOY) is valued at €294.4M (~$341M).

Cevian said it "sees significant long-term value potential in Akzo Nobel... We have high expectations that Chair Ben Noteboom and [CEO] Greg Poux-Guillaume will deliver on their strategy of improving operating performance and sharpening the portfolio to realize this potential."

Akzo Nobel (OTCQX:AKZOF) (OTCQX:AKZOY) is in a strategic review of its portfolio as it copes with weakness in its core decorative paints and coatings markets, and recently agreed to sell its Indian unit in a deal valued at €1.4B including debt.

"We've had regular contact with Cevian over the past two years and welcome their interest in Akzo Nobel," a company spokesman said.

data.title: LendingTree announces $475M credit facility
data.description:
LendingTree (NASDAQ:TREE) announced on Friday a $475M credit facility, consisting of a $400M term loan B and a $75M revolving credit facility with five-year maturities.

Shares were +1.19% pre-market to $63.90.

The $400M loan carries an interest rate of SOFR + 450 bps and the $75M revolver an interest rate of SOFR + 350 bps.

Proceeds are expected to be used to refinance existing debt and for general corporate purposes.

The financing replaces the company's existing term loan B due 2028 and a loan agreement with Apollo.

"The new facility will reduce our interest expense, eliminate several restrictive covenants from prior agreements, and reward shareholders with a lower cost of capital that is reflective of our improved growth trajectory," said CFO Jason Bengel.

data.title: Zoom perks up as analysts identify healthy outlook, AI adoption trends
data.description:
Zoom's (NASDAQ:ZM) third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results featured the highest year-over-year revenue growth in 11 quarters, bolstered by its Enteprise segment and artificial intelligence tools.

The stock climbed 4% during early Friday trading as most financial firms came away impressed by the results and outlook.

"Enterprise rev of $730.7m (+7% y/y) was $12.3m ahead of consensus (1.7% upside)," said Jefferies analysts, led by Samad Samana, in a Friday investor note. "The firm is continuing to benefit from ARPU expansion into large enterprises, with $100k+ in TTM rev customers modestly accelerating to +9% y/y (32% of total rev). From a product standpoint, $100k+ ARR Workvivo customers grew to 168 (+142% y/y) and $100k + ARR ZCC customers grew to 229 (+94% y/y)."

Jefferies maintained its Buy rating and $100 price target.

Similarly, Wedbush reiterated its Outperform rating and $95 price target following the results.

"ZM AI Companion remains a strong spot in the business with MAUs growing 4x y/y as this feature provides new and improved AI use cases built into the platform with enterprises now expanding their AI use cases beyond basic meeting summaries with more momentum for its AI integration across multiple products including Zoom Phone and Zoom Docs," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a note. 

Meanwhile, Oppenheimer maintained its Perform rating, but did not assign a price target. They noted Zoom's transition to an "AI-first" company appears to be working.

"AI Companion's usage is extending beyond meeting summaries to include answering users' questions, scheduling and preparing meetings, searching information, and building content," said Oppenheimer analyst Timothy Horan, in a note. "ZM is monetizing AI through its Custom AI Companion offering, which is reportedly earning positive customer reception."

Wells Fargo retained its Hold rating on the stock and an $80 price target as they are unsure if Zoom can maintain its growth reacceleration.

Stifel also maintained its Hold rating but reduced its price target to $80 from $85.

"Zoom has effectively seeded the market with AI functionalities, with 'millions' of AI Companion users, and continues to ramp monetization, most meaningfully through Contact Center Elite and ZVA today," said Stifel anlaysts, led by J. Parker Lane, in a note. "Finally, while management noted that macro conditions remain dynamic, the company observed a partial abatement of the heightened scrutiny in certain geographies that was noted in 1Q."

Zoom competes against much larger companies that offer video conferecing platforms, such as Microsoft (MSFT) Teams, Cisco's (CSCO) Webex and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) Meet. Shares for these stocks were up 0.1%, 0.5% and 1.5%, respectively.

Zoom's annual flagship event, Zoomtopia, is scheduled to run September 17-18.
